festivals.name: Jerash Festival of Culture and Arts
festivals.description: International performers stage concerts and theater inside one of the best-preserved Roman cities in the world.

festivals.name: Wadi Rum Trekking Season
festivals.description: April's mild temperatures make it prime season for multi-day trekking and camping among Wadi Rum's red sandstone canyons.

destinations.name: Petra
destinations.description: The Rose-Red City carved from sandstone cliffs by the Nabataean Arabs 2,000 years ago — one of the New Seven Wonders.

destinations.name: Wadi Rum
destinations.description: A UNESCO-protected valley of red sandstone and granite where Bedouin camps and Martian landscapes meet extraordinary stars.

destinations.name: Amman
destinations.description: Jordan's hilly capital blends a 6,000-year history — the Roman Citadel and Amphitheatre — with a vibrant modern cafe culture.

destinations.name: Dead Sea
destinations.description: The lowest point on earth at 430m below sea level, where the salt concentration lets you float effortlessly.

destinations.name: Jerash
destinations.description: Among the best-preserved Roman provincial cities in the world, with intact colonnaded streets and triumphal arches.

destinations.name: Wadi Mujib
destinations.description: Jordan's Grand Canyon offers exhilarating canyon swims through a UNESCO biosphere reserve dropping to the Dead Sea.

destinations.name: Aqaba
destinations.description: Jordan's only coastal city on the Red Sea offers world-class coral reef diving and year-round warm waters.

destinations.name: Madaba & Mount Nebo
destinations.description: Madaba's Byzantine mosaic map of the Holy Land and Moses's mountaintop with its panoramic view of the Promised Land.
